name: Kangaita Tea Estate, East Rift Valley, Kenya (~2180m)

staple_foods.name: Kenya highland green tea (Camellia sinensis)

key_flavor_bioactives.polyphenol_content: 18.7 g/100g dry matter total catechins (EGCG 7.9%, ECG 3.3%, EGC 5.2%, EC 1.5%, C 0.7%; mean of 60 clones, fresh leaf steamed and dried — Mutuku et al. 2016 Am J Plant Sci 7:562, doi:10.4236/ajps.2016.73049)
key_flavor_bioactives.caffeine_concentration: 3.0-4.0 g/100g dry matter [Kenyan highland tea caffeine estimate]

umami_compounds.glutamate: 0.8-1.5 g/100g dry matter as theanine [Kenyan highland tea estimate]

staple_foods.cultivar: Camellia sinensis — 60 clones including Kenyan commercial cultivars (AHP, TRFK series); mean across clone panel
staple_foods.notes: Kangaita Tea Factory, Kirinyaga County, East Rift Valley, Kenya (Mount Kenya foothills). Altitude 2180m. Data from Mutuku et al. (2016): fresh leaf steamed and dried to green tea equivalent, HPLC catechin quantification, mean across 60 clones. Total catechins 18.7 g/100g DM — highest in the Mutuku study, consistent with altitude. No shade-growing mentioned. Paired with RSU-64 (Timbilil, West Rift Valley, 2020m) from the same study, providing within-study cross-rift comparison. Close in altitude to RSU-58 (Kericho, ~2200m) but independent source and location.

notes: Kangaita Tea Factory, Kirinyaga County, East Rift Valley, Kenya. Located at ~2180m on the southern slopes of Mount Kenya. KTDA-operated factory catchment. Paired with Timbilil (West Rift, 2020m) in Mutuku et al. (2016) to compare catechin profiles across Kenya's two main tea-growing rifts. Higher altitude and slightly higher catechin content than Timbilil, consistent with the altitude gradient. Complements RSU-58 (Kericho, ~2200m, Kim et al. 2011) as an independent high-altitude Kenya data point from a different geographic sub-region.
data_sources: Mutuku A et al. (2016) — Influence of geographical regions on catechin and caffeine levels in tea (Camellia sinensis). American Journal of Plant Sciences 7(3):562-571. doi:10.4236/ajps.2016.73049.
